product.problem: Backend teams need a small webhook ingestion service that safely receives partner callbacks, normalizes incoming events, and reliably hands them to internal processing.
target_users: Backend engineers integrating partner webhook providers
Platform teams that need a reusable ingestion boundary before internal queue processing
goals: Expose one HTTP endpoint for partner webhook ingestion
Verify inbound webhook authenticity with HMAC SHA-256 signatures
Normalize accepted payloads into a stable internal event envelope
Deliver events to a provider-neutral queue adapter with bounded retry and dead-letter handling
non_goals: No provider-specific queue SDK in v1
No long-term event storage beyond the dead-letter path
core_flows: A partner sends a signed webhook request to the ingestion endpoint
The service verifies timestamp tolerance and HMAC signature before parsing the payload
The service normalizes the payload into an internal event envelope with provider, event type, event id, and received timestamp
The queue adapter accepts the event or the service retries according to the bounded policy
Exhausted deliveries are written to the dead-letter path for manual inspection
screens_or_interfaces: Queue adapter interface for enqueue and dead-letter operations
Configuration object for partner secrets, timestamp tolerance, and retry settings
data_model_draft: WebhookRequest: partner id, headers, raw body, receivedAt
NormalizedEvent: eventId, partner, eventType, payload, receivedAt
DeadLetterRecord: normalized event, attempts, final error
external_integrations: Partner webhook sender using shared-secret HMAC signatures
success_criteria: Unsigned or incorrectly signed requests are rejected
Accepted requests produce normalized event envelopes
Transient queue failures are retried with a bounded policy
Exhausted delivery attempts are sent to the dead-letter path
Signature, normalization, retry, and dead-letter paths have deterministic tests
constraints: HMAC SHA-256 signatures with timestamp tolerance
No concrete cloud provider dependency in v1

architecture: Use a small TypeScript HTTP service with request body access before JSON parsing for signature verification
Separate request verification, payload normalization, queue delivery, retry policy, and dead-letter handling into modules
Keep queue integration behind an adapter interface so tests can use deterministic fakes
data_flow: HTTP handler reads raw body and partner id
Verifier checks timestamp tolerance and HMAC SHA-256 signature
Normalizer maps provider payload to a stable internal event envelope
Delivery service calls QueueAdapter.enqueue and applies bounded retry on transient failures
DeadLetterAdapter records exhausted deliveries with final error context
dependencies: Node.js HTTP framework or minimal server
Test runner with fake queue and fake clock support
verification: Unit tests for valid and invalid HMAC signatures
Unit tests for bounded retry and dead-letter behavior
Handler tests for accepted, rejected, and malformed webhook requests

clarifying_question_disposition.rationale: Webhook authenticity affects security and endpoint semantics, so it is tracked as a formal Gate B decision.
clarifying_question_disposition.resolution: Use HMAC SHA-256 signatures with shared partner secrets and timestamp tolerance in v1

clarifying_question_disposition.rationale: The retry guarantee is resolved by the selected bounded retry plus dead-letter intake decision.
clarifying_question_disposition.resolved_by: ND-3 answer: Bounded retry plus dead-letter path

evidence.title: Answered intake decisions and Gate B signature decision
evidence.used_for: Confirmed TypeScript runtime, provider-neutral queue adapter, bounded retry behavior, and HMAC SHA-256 signature verification.

evidence.title: Node.js Introduction
evidence.used_for: Grounded the TypeScript/Node.js HTTP-service runtime choice used by the Gate B implementation plan.

approval_audit.approval_note: Approved after resolving ND-4 signature verification and confirming no open decisions remain.
